what_it_does: This file stores a minimal YAML-frontmatter metadata record that registers an image attachment for a design-source channel memory entry.
observations: The entire content is YAML frontmatter with no body text.
It declares an attachment of type images.
The target is an image://design-source/company-info/p-01 URI.
It includes an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format.
No code symbols, captions, or descriptive text are present.
standouts: The target uses an image:// URI scheme rather than a filesystem path.
The file is named att-<uuid>.md and lives in a channel_attachments directory.
It ties the attachment to the design-source channel.
concerns: There is no caption, alt text, or description of the image.
The image:// URI cannot be verified or resolved from this file alone.
No author or source information is recorded.
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that points an image asset at a design-source channel memory record.
questions: What does the image p-01 actually depict?
Who added this attachment and why?
How is this attachment related to a specific channel memory entry?
What are the image format, size, and storage location?
intention_signals: It appears intended to catalog an image asset as an attachment linked to the design-source channel so it can be retrieved alongside related memory/context.
novelty: The use of an image:// URI scheme as the attachment target and the typed attachment_type field in a Markdown ledger file.
contribution: It contributes a typed, timestamped image attachment reference bound to the design-source channel, which a fused system could use to locate or surface the associated visual asset.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger file named att-3bc61825.md records an image attachment targeting image://design-source/company-info/p-01 for the design-source channel.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to register an image asset as an attachment to a design-source channel memory entry.
